title: Pods, Multi-Container Composition, Lifecycle, Termination, and Debug Containers · Reconciliation Brief: K8s Hooks Signals Termination
summary: An accessible status service shares a Pod with initialization and support containers but loses requests during termination and hides its first crash. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s hooks signals termination increment in bounded Pod composition and lifecycle manifest. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design preStop, stop signals, termination grace, endpoint removal, application draining, child cleanup, and forced termination evidence.
Trace waiting, running, terminated, reasons, exit codes, restartPolicy, backoff, previous state, and Pod phase without equating them.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Hooks Signals Termination · Theory K8s Hooks Signals Termination
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Pod has an init container that never completes, a sidecar that outlives the app, an ephemeral debug container, restart count 4, and an emptyDir holding the only diagnosis. Explain phase, container state, restart, sharing, and replacement boundaries. Competency probe: Design preStop, stop signals, termination grace, endpoint removal, application draining, child cleanup, and forced termination evidence.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the K8s Hooks Signals Termination contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Pod has an init container that never completes, a sidecar that outlives the app, an ephemeral debug container, restart count 4, and an emptyDir holding the only diagnosis. Explain phase, container state, restart, sharing, and replacement boundaries. Competency probe: Design preStop, stop signals, termination grace, endpoint removal, application draining, child cleanup, and forced termination evidence. A defensible solution applies K8s Hooks Signals Termination, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to bounded Pod composition and lifecycle manifest.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-pods-lifecycle-composition-4
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-pods-lifecycle-composition-4
    artifact: pod.yaml
    decision: worked k8s pods lifecycle composition 4
    environment: air-gapped release cluster using an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: Pod
      metadata:
      name: status-api
      spec:
      initContainers: [schema-check]
      containers: [api, telemetry-sidecar]
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
    verify: kubectl get pod status-api -o json && kubectl logs status-api --previous -c api
    failure: Reject durable-Pod assumptions, unbounded sidecars, swallowed signals, missing previous logs, or debug mutation presented as image repair.
    repair: Assign container ownership, bound startup and shutdown, capture states and exit causes, and prove a clean replacement preserves required behavior.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A preStop hook replaces application signal handling and always runs before every termination. This fails the K8s Hooks Signals Termination contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
